codeReview.summary: Implementation adds a preset dropdown to SimulatorCore that allows loading 14 pre-built example programs. When a preset is selected, the code is loaded into the editor and CPU registers are reset to initial state. The dropdown displays example filename, difficulty badge, and instruction count.

issues.description: Lint warning: 'examples' is unnecessary dependency in loadPreset useCallback. The linter suggests removing it since examples.find() doesn't mutate examples. However, this is a minor style warning and does not affect functionality.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used existing import pattern `@/data/processed/examples.json` which was already established in the codebase (ExampleExplorer.tsx uses the same pattern to import examplesData). No new conventions introduced.
Worker used the `cn` utility from `@/lib/utils` which is already used in other simulator components (SimulatorControls.tsx, RegisterDisplay.tsx). This follows existing pattern.
No new services or commands were started. Worker used existing npm scripts (build, lint) and Playwright browser automation for testing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows no new services.yaml entries required

summary: Code review passed. The preset dropdown feature is implemented correctly with proper dropdown UI showing 14 examples, code loading functionality, and register reset on preset selection. TypeScript compiles without errors. Browser testing confirmed the feature works end-to-end (dropdown opens, preset selected, code loads, registers reset). One non-blocking lint warning about unnecessary dependency in useCallback - does not affect functionality.
